docs.title: uh halp
docs.text:
Install:

pip install uh-halp

Configure:

$ uh where am i?
Need an OpenAI key, it'll be saved to ~/.uh-key: <paste here>
pwd

Usage:

$ uh check my syslog
tail -f /var/log/syslog

$ uh clean up docker disk space
docker system prune -a

$ uh sync this shit to /home/gaz/project on gaz@server
rsync -avz --progress . gaz@server:/home/gaz/project

$ uh how much space have I got free here?
df -h .

$ uh gimme a socks proxy on 192.168.0.1
ssh -D 8080 username@192.168.0.1

$ uh check if the internet is up
ping -c 3 google.com

$ uh make an annoying beeping noise at random intervals forever in the background
while true; do echo -e "\a"; sleep $((RANDOM % 10)); done &

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
OpenAI are bawbags. Expect crying if you ask it to do anything naughty or swear at it.

It's using the cheapest model and will get things wrong, so y'know, look before you paste.

Lacks testing, works_on_my_machine.jpg. Should also work in Windows as the shell and OS details get passed to the system prompt. But it might not!

docs.title: Disclaimer
docs.text: If it blows your machine up it's your own fault. Don't run code produced by a language model without reading it, their breadth of knowledge is matched only by their depth of stupidity.

docs.title: License
docs.text: WTFPL with one additional clause: don't blame me!

docs.title: get_os
docs.text:
def get_os()

Returns a string describing the OS.

docs.title: get_shell
docs.text:
def get_shell()

Returns the user's shell executable name.

docs.title: info
docs.text:
def info()

Returns the OS info string that's used as the system message.

docs.title: get_sys_msg
docs.text:
def get_sys_msg()

Returns the system message sent to the LLM, asking it nicely to return a program.

docs.title: clean
docs.text:
def clean(response: str)

Cleans the response from the API, because we can't trust it to play nicely.

def query()

Queries the OpenAI API and returns the result

docs.text:
def get_key()

Loads the OpenAI key from ~/.uh-key, or prompts for it and saves it there.
